description:
From textured crops to stick-straight lobs, blunt hairstyles are classic. Plus, they're celeb-approved, with stars like Scarlett Johansson, Lori Harvey, and Lucy Liu donning the style. Each one opted for a distinct blunt cut on their differently textured hair, and each one looked fabulous. Even iconic curly girls like Yara Shahidi and Zazie Beetz showed the masses how to eschew layers without sacrificing their natural texture.
To find out more about the most flattering blunt haircuts for your face shape and hair texture, keep scrolling for 26 interpretations—complete with pro styling tips from Valles and King.

Kevin Mazur/MG23/Getty Images for The Met Museum/Vogue
Joan Smalls's straight, blunt lob cuts like a knife. Chic and edgy, it will elevate any look—not to mention, flatter any hair type thanks to its shoulder-grazing length. Just make sure to work in ahair oilto keep frizz at bay and shine maximized.

Pascal Le Segretain/Getty Images
The adorable Zazie Beetz proves that blunt cuts can work on all hair types and textures. Hitting just above the chin, this bob draws attention to Beetz's killer bone structure and strong jawline. But to keep her waves styled just right,moisture is key.
"If you havecurly hair, researching hairstylists and going to a stylist you trust would be the most beneficial to avoid your hair from looking too boxy with this cut," recommends Valles.

Presley Ann/Getty Images for UnitedMasters
When looking for hairspiration, model Soo Joo Park never disappoints. Here, her trendingjellyfishblunt haircut is doubly fierce and head-turning. Bleached brows add a nice color contrast.

Pierre Suu/Getty Images
As much as short hair suits Joey King, this shoulder-length, blunt cut with bangs might just be even more stunning. The lash-grazing bangs highlight her eyes while the sombré color adds dimension. What more could you ask for? (In case you were wondering, sombré stands for soft ombré.)

@yarashahidi
While super coily hair textures tend to avoid blunt cuts and opt for lighter layers that reduce the weight of their curls, Yara Shahidi proves that a layerless stylecanbe an option. The shorter length is key, offering spring-enhancing levity without a choppier cut.

Jamie McCarthy/Getty Images for alice + olivia
Nothing fancy here, but boy, is the haircut intriguing. Lucy Hale's lob is tousled ever so slightly to lend some volume on top and give it an air of not trying too hard. "The illusion behind this cut is to give your hair a thicker, fuller appearance," Valles says.
"Blunt cuts can flatter any face shape by adjusting the length of the cut. Longer [is better] for more oval faces and shorter for more heart-shaped orsquare faces," says King.

Gareth Cattermole/Getty Images
Scarlett Johansson is serving up Old Hollywood glam with her retro waves, but blunt ends lend a modern feel. "There are many different styles of blunt haircuts that can go with specific face shapes," says Valles.Heart face shapeswill look great with a longer blunt cut since it complements a narrower chin, she adds.

Steve Granitz/FilmMagic
Those with oval face shapes can do no wrong when it comes to experimenting with all types of haircuts, blunt ones included. And thisslicked-back styleon Laura Harrier is one to fawn over—repeatedly. FYI: Gel is a must for this look to stay put and smooth.

Stephane Cardinale - Corbis/Corbis via Getty Images
It doesn't have to be an all-or-nothing game. If letting go of those beloved layers provokes an identity crisis, you can always fake your way through it. Lea Haddad's luscious, curly mane no doubt employs some invisible layers to lighten the load, but the overall appearance gives the illusion of the same sharp, heavy-ended finish created by blunt cuts.

Daniele Venturelli/WireImage/Getty Images
Characterized by a sliced, chin-length cut, the sophisticated French bob is another curly-girl blunt-cut loophole. While the ultra-modern, pin-straight iteration may be the first image to come to mind, its more rumpled little sister pairs seamlessly with the natural texture of curls and coils. Maria Borges's fringed, wet-look style offers a refreshing approach.

Steve Granitz/FilmMagic
January Jones's wavy texture is hair envy-inducing. Consider this blunt lob if you've got fine strands, since it creates movement and body. And if you're curious about bangs, these low-maintenancecurtain bangswill ease you into fringe.

Samir Hussein/WireImage
Blunt haircuts can be short or they can be long, as Jennifer Connelly demonstrates here. "It can really be any length," says King. "It creates a clean, sharp line." This is especially pronounced when styled straight. The longer length really works for Connelly. "If you have a square face shape, it is best to avoid blunt hairstyles such as single-length bobs and straight bangs," notes Valles.

Peter White/Getty Images
A blunt bob with bangs is the perfect face-framing look. It also serves up extra drama when styled straight. With its bangs and short length, this cut is especially flattering for oblong faces and/or those with a larger forehead and thinner hair density.

Michael Loccisano/Getty Images
Sometimes, simple is best. Lucy Liu's bob is a classic cut for a reason. Her chin-length bob is face-framing and lovely. The slightly off-center part keeps it casual. "If you have thinner hair, you should most likely choose a shorter blunt cut option to give your hair a fuller, thicker look," says Valles.

Karwai Tang/WireImage/Getty Images
A blunt haircut with long, loose waves lends body to fine hair. It also elongates a rounder face shape, as seen on Emma Stone here. The result is a red carpet-ready look that is elegant and polished.

Paras Griffin/Getty Images for BET
There is close to nothing that Keke Palmer can’t do—she can act, sing, dance, do comedy, and pick a darn good haircut. The slightly inward-turned ends of her copper bob help to frame her diamond-shaped face while elevating her overall look. The barely there natural roots make it look more down-to-earth and approachable.

Stephane Cardinale - Corbis/Corbis via Getty Images
A long, blunt haircut is incredibly versatile. Here, Naomi Campbell wears hers partly pulled back and into a fun, fanned-out design. Twoface-framing tendrilsadd some '90s appeal.

Leon Bennett/Getty Images for Ebony Media Group
Whether you're growing out your French bob or going in for the chop, a blunt bob is simple yet statement-making. While you can style it with waves, Meagan Good goes for a classic, straight iteration that doesn't disappoint. "I think the most important thing is to find a great haircutter to execute the look. Then you can wear it however you like…wavy or straight," says King.

Michael Loccisano/Getty Images
When worn straight, a blunt haircut is always eye-catching. "All hair types and textures can achieve the blunt haircut look. It is more about the technique and who is cutting/styling your hair than the type of hair you have," says Valles. Since Alexandra Daddario's hair is medium to thick density, she opts for a longer length to best flatter her hair texture.

Steve Granitz/FilmMagic
Add some fun flair to your blunt cut with your hair accessory of choice. Here, America Ferrera packages her tucked bobwith a bow. If you decide to go for a bob, you'll need to stay on top of trims (unless you're growing it out, that is). "A commitment to a shorter length means a little more upkeep to maintain the style. You may want toconsider a trimevery couple of months," says King.

David Crotty/Getty Images
A hair flip and subtle waves switch up the vibe of Charlize Theron's blunt bob, which suits those with naturally straight, fine hair. Hitting at the chin, this length works well on oval or oblong face shapes. For a heart-shaped face, consider slightly flipping out the ends of your hair to create width—and balance—at the chin.
"Research whattype of face shapeyou have if you don’t know already," says Valles. There are many different types of blunt haircuts, so knowing which one would look good on you to show inspo pictures to your stylist would be beneficial."

Stephane Cardinale - Corbis/Corbis via Getty Images
Cool blonde highlights add interest and dimension to Kelly Rowland's super-straight blunt cut. It's also a lesson in how to wearhighlights with a dark hair color—and feigning thicker hair density, too. "If you have thinner hair, you may want to get a shorter cut in order to give your hair a fuller look," says Valles.

Gareth Cattermole/Getty Images
As stunning as it is when Dame Helen Mirren experiments with color (remember her blue look?), this gray style is especially gorgeous. Simple and sophisticated, tucking hair behind her ears lends a polished finish.

Aliah Anderson/WireImage
Maya Rudolph's sophisticated lob falls just below the chin, elongating and complementing herround face shape. "For a round face, blunt [long] bobs that are sleek and straight add more to the face’s dimension and structure," says Valles. She recommends wearing a middle part to flatter and frame a rounder shape.

Jacopo Raule/GC Images
Even though curls may not seem like they lend themselves to blunt haircuts, there's a way to make the natural texture shine without layers. The key? Letting strategic shaping (in this case, angling) and precise ends do the heavy lifting.

Taylor Hill/FilmMagic
This blunt bob on Ciara proved that a great haircut can help you radiate confidence. It's best for people who already have thick hair, because it'll look fuller, but folks with thin hair can also feign the volume. Just make sure you use a great blow dryer and sometexturizing spray.

Love it or hate it: theside-partis having a comeback as of late (and the millennials are jumping for joy!). From sweet, curled-under styles to tousled waves, the off-centered part will do more for your thin hair than you think. “The side part bob is a great hack for faking fullness, we tend to tuck the side with less hair behind our ear which condenses the hair together giving the illusion of fuller hair,” says Schulz. “And the other side simply looks fuller because the majority of your hair is to that side. Add in some soft waves for added fullness.”

To nail the flippy bob, à la Kendall Jenner, you’ll want aDyson, a Shark, or even just a classic round brush on hand. This playful, girly bob is all about movement–flip out the ends (a flatiron works just as well, too!) for that signature bounce. Schulz also recommends securing the front pieces back with clips to instantly increase the look of the density.

If you’re looking for a bob that instantly adds movement to thin hair, a textured version is a stylist favorite. The cut incorporates soft, airy layers that lend the style a slightly tousled—yet intentional—finish while helping strands appear fuller. “This is a bob that’s likely been cut with a razor, it adds lots of fun and flirty texture to the style,” says Schulz, noting that the lighter layers also help create natural-looking volume. A trustytexture spraycan also help enhance the shape and keep things in place. For those wanting even more body, Destin says a modern perm can help add long-lasting texture to fine hair.

Calling all cool girls to the front.Bixies–a hybrid of a bob and a pixie–is the ultimate way to stand out, and it’s especially flattering for those with thinner hair. The cut keeps a bob-like perimeter in the back while incorporating shorter, face-framing pieces in the front, creating soft fringe and layers long enough to tuck behind the ears. Style it with loose waves and finish with a texture putty to define the shorter pieces and add dimension.

French bobswill never go out of style–and as Schulz puts it, “the French quite literally invented the word chic”. This ultra-cropped cut is typically falls between the cheekbone and jawline, giving it that signature effortless look. Because the length is so short and the cut features minimal layers or texture, it makes things look denser than they appear.

Stars like Ciara and Dua Lipa have made theblunt boba modern staple. This chic, cool-girl cut features hair trimmed to one uniform length, creating a sleek and polished finish. Because there are no layers, the straight-across shape helps fine hair look fuller.

Also dubbed as the Brad bob, this ‘90s heartthrob-inspired style proved that not every bob has to fall directly in your face. The cut is tapered and layered toward the back–behind the ears where hair tends to have most density–while leaving slightly longer pieces in the front for easy movement and face-framing shape. “For thin hair, the ‘90s Brad can even be permed to add a bit of body and lift to the hair,” says Destin. Wear it softly blown out, sleek and silk-pressed, or styled with a bit of product for a more controlled finish.

For thin hair, a graduated bob is a flattering choice that accentuates the neckline while creating a cool, polished effect. The timeless cut features stacked layers in the back that give the style a youthful edge and a flirty swing. This subtle structure helps create the illusion of fuller, denser hair. Adding highlights can enhance the look even more, bringing lift, dimension, and movement to the shape.

This style works best with a jaw-length bob. When the hair is tucked behind the ears, it naturally creates a soft C-shape that hugs the jawline and frames the face beautifully. To achieve the look, use a flatiron to gently curve the ends under before tucking behind your ears. Think flippy bob, but inwards!

If you have thin hair and natural curls, a bob can instantly make your strands appear thicker. Cutting curls shorter often helps them spring up more, creating extra volume and shape throughout the style. Adding a textured bang can also help frame the face and soften the overall look.

Other inclusions show Lohan making chocolate bark with pomegranate seeds, pumpkin seeds, and coconut flakes. She added another makeup-free selfie with "derm check" written overtop and a smiley picture of herself and Shammas in matching brown jackets and dark shades.
Lindsay Lohan / Instagram
"Lately 🤍," Lohanwrote below the post.
Luai might be in his toddler years, but Lohan is still in baby bliss. The couple is committed to protecting their son's privacy (which was a big reason for their move to Dubai), but Lohan shares glimpses of him every now and then, including a recent gushing quote when shecalled Luai her "greatest joy."

The last time Berry had cheek-skimming strands was in 2023. She stepped out at the 95th Academy Awards with a super-short bob styled in a deep side part with retro crimped curls.
Getty Images
The bob-pixie hybrid called back to one of Berry's most iconic roles: Leticia Musgrove inMonster's Ball. Fans won't need reminding that Berry had a fluffy, grown-out brunette pixie with full bangs for the 2001 film.
She trimmed that pixie even shorter the next year, rocking tufts of hair close to her scalp, to accept the Oscar for Best Actress for her role in the romance-thriller.

The Recovery Shampoois designed to repair damaged hair, with Virtue's patented Alpha Keratin 60ku—a protein described by the brand as “identical to the keratin that makes up our hair”—doing a lot of the heavy lifting. It’s pretty science heavy (you can getmore into the weeds here), but the TL;DR is this: The ingredient fills in areas of damage to make once brittle strands strong and soft. It’s not sexy, but I think of it as caulk for broken hair.
The claims are certainly big, but fans guarantee that this shampoo lives up to all of its promises.One shoppersaid this turned their “weak, dry…hair into silk,” whileanotherraved that it made their “straw”-like strands “shiny and healthy.” Another reviewer on Virtue’s website said that “in just two weeks” their hair became “so much stronger and softer.”
This doesn’t stop at just making strands softer, though. By repairing damage,Virtue’s Recovery Shampoolays the groundwork formore growth. According to other reviews on the brand site, someone whose length was stagnant switched to this shampoo; their hair is now “half [way] down [their] back” with “no split ends in sight.” Another user immediately noticed that their mane felt thicker after just a few washes, and, “before the bottle was empty,” saw that their “hair started growing back.” It’s not overnight results, but, come on, it’s notthatmuch longer.
So if you feel that your strands could use a little TLC,Virtue’s Recovery Shampoomay need to be your next buy. Below, shop more of the products used on Kidman, including thedamage-reversing serumandHealing Oilshe’s usedsince 2024.

In a September 2020 Instagram post, the actress called her gray hairs “the white hair of wisdom,” and in 2023,she cheekily shared a tipfor how “to cover your white hair without dyeing it,” she wrote alongside a photo of herself wearing sunglasses in her hair, which showed off her gray hair.
“Don’t wear your glasses on your hair!!!” she joked. “Bring on the wisdom.”
In an appearance onGood Morning America, Hayek wasn’t afraid to joke about her grays once more, telling the hosts, “You don’t see the white hair right now because I put mascara [on].”
“Because I’m too lazy to dye my hair, so I go like this and then I go like this,” she added, showing the audience the motion of not only putting mascara on her eyelashes but also on her hair framing her face.
Hayek has opened up over the years about howshe doesn’t believe in Botox, and how she’s embracing aging as she inches closer to 60—the age she’ll turn later this year. “I used to criticize myself so much,” Hayek previously toldPeople. “Now when I look in the mirror and there are things that I don’t see, I think about how much I’m going to like me in 10 years.”
“I’m an actress, a mother, and a wife—I want my expressions to be authentic,” she toldThe Cutof avoiding procedures like Botox. “I want my kids to look at my face and it’s very clear when I’m proud of them and clear when I’m not happy. I’m also an actress—I need the expressions on my face.”
Of aging, she toldGlamour, “I thought getting older meant I wasn’t going to work—I’m working. I thought getting older maybe meant that you’re not in love anymore—I’m in love.”
“I don’t feel that I lost my flexibility or my agility or even my strength,” she continued, adding of her husband François-Henri Pinault, “I do have to say that I have found it beautiful, getting older with someone.”
As for how Hollywood looks at aging these days, “Yeah, it’s changing,” Hayek said. “I was also told that, as an actress, you kind of stop working around 30. Imagine? I did really feel it at the time, and it’s unfair. If I had said instead to myself, ‘Don’t worry, your best professional moments are going to be in your 50s,’ I would have been devastated. ‘Oh my God, do I have to wait that long?’ But it’ll happen.”
Hayek added, “Hell, I’m still going. Shocker. I’m still here. They tried to get rid of me a thousand ways—I’m still here.”
See the latest celebrity-inspired hairstyles, haircuts, and color ideas. We share the latest runway trends; haircuts for your face shape, product reviews, and more.

Longoria let the sleek, middle-parted bob speak for itself, completing the look with natural makeup, a rosy pink lip, and a beige quarter-zip. From all angles, Longoria's bob shone, reminding us just how slick a bold bob can be. (Of course, we'll still be taking the choppy bob,the micro bob with bangs, and the classic, sophisticated bob into consideration all season long.)
Eva Longoria/Instagram
In October 2025, after years of sporting lengthy waves, Longoria teased her first step toward the dramatic chop. While in London to celebrate longtime friend Victoria Beckham's eponymous Netflix docuseries, theDesperate Housewivesalum rocked a face-framing lob, joining the dozens of celebrities who have tried on the chic hairstyle.
Of course, the voluminous lob was just a taste of what was to come from Longoria and her hairstyling team. Historically, the actress has been pretty consistent with her hairstyling choices. If the last few months are any indicator, it seems like Longoria has entered a new era of daring hair choices, and we are completely here for it.
With spring and summer inching closer, we can't wait to see what unexpected haircuts she surprises us with next.
Getty Images
